circular_title: GST Advisory: New Invoice Management System (IMS) - April 2026
circular_summary: Invoice Management System mandatory from April 1, 2026 for all GST filers.
actions: Review supplier-uploaded invoices within the IMS workflow before auto-population into GSTR-2B and take necessary actions (accept, reject, or keep pending) within the prescribed cycle
Reconcile purchase register with IMS actions to ensure accurate reflection of eligible input tax credit in GSTR-3B
Follow up with vendors before filing GSTR-3B to ensure claim of input tax credit matches accepted invoice data
email_subject: Compliance Advisory: GST Advisory: New Invoice Management System (IMS) - April 2026
email_body:
{
  "subject": "GST Advisory: New Invoice Management System (IMS) - April 2026",
  "body": "Amit Verma, 

As a stock broker, Galaxy Securities Pvt. Ltd. is required to comply with the new GST Invoice Management System (IMS) to ensure accurate input tax credit claims. This applies to your company specifically because you deal with numerous vendors and suppliers, making it essential to manage invoices effectively.

To comply with the new IMS, please take the following actions:
1. Review supplier-uploaded invoices within the IMS workflow before auto-population into GSTR-2B and take necessary actions (accept, reject, or keep pending) within the prescribed cycle.
2. Reconcile purchase register with IMS actions to ensure accurate reflection of eligible input tax credit in GSTR-3B.
3. Follow up with vendors before filing GSTR-3B to ensure claim of input tax credit matches accepted invoice data.

The deadline for compliance is April 20, 2026. Although the penalty for non-compliance is not specified, it is crucial to adhere to the deadline to avoid any potential risks.

Please do not hesitate to contact us if you require any assistance with the new IMS. We are here to help you navigate the compliance process.

Best regards,
Compliance Advisory Team"
}
internal_notes: CA notes — deadline was estimated based on monthly GSTR-3B filing, cross-checks needed for accurate input tax credit claim
